tossups.question: This artist created a series of alabaster self-portraits titled Five Stages of Maya Dance. A Tumblr blog called “[this artist] Made Me Cry” features photos by Marco Anelli captioned with the amount of time it took for visitors to an exhibition by this artist to cry. This artist interviewed a rat-catcher for one of their works inspired by a certain war and requiring thousands of cow bones. This artist was denied permission to reproduce (*) Burden’s Trans-Fixed for their Seven Easy Pieces. An audience member placed this artist’s finger on the trigger of a gun pointed at their head in a work in which visitors were presented with 72 objects to use on them. For 10 points, Rhythm 0 was created by which Serbian artist, who had visitors line up to sit at a table opposite her in The Artist Is Present?
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist created a series of alabaster self-portraits titled Five Stages of Maya Dance. A Tumblr blog called "[this artist] Made Me Cry" features photos by Marco Anelli captioned with the amount of time it took for visitors to an exhibition by this artist to cry. This artist interviewed a rat-catcher for one of their works inspired by a certain war and requiring thousands of cow bones. This artist was denied permission to reproduce (*) Burden's Trans-Fixed for their Seven Easy Pieces. An audience member placed this artist's finger on the trigger of a gun pointed at their head in a work in which visitors were presented with 72 objects to use on them. For 10 points, Rhythm 0 was created by which Serbian artist, who had visitors line up to sit at a table opposite her in The Artist Is Present?
tossups.answer_sanitized: Marina Abramovic ("uh-BRAM-o-vitch") [accept Marina Abramovic Made Me Cry]

tossups.question: Denmark’s Louisiana Museum of Art hosted this artist’s first European retrospective in 2017, titled The Cleaner. This artist invited John Podesta to a spirit cooking in an email leaked during the 2016 election, which led Alex Jones to link this artist and Hillary Clinton to the occult. This artist paired Entering the Other Side with pieces by Bruce Nauman and Joseph Beuys in Seven Easy Pieces. This artist and their partner met in the middle of the Great Wall of China to end their relationship in the piece Lovers. A fight broke out when an (*) audience member held a gun to this artist’s head in Rhythm 0. Lady Gaga helped popularize interest in this artist’s performance at a 2010 MoMA retrospective in which this artist sat in silence across from museum visitors. For 10 points, name this Serbian “grandmother of performance art” whose work includes The Artist is Present.
tossups.question_sanitized: Denmark's Louisiana Museum of Art hosted this artist's first European retrospective in 2017, titled The Cleaner. This artist invited John Podesta to a spirit cooking in an email leaked during the 2016 election, which led Alex Jones to link this artist and Hillary Clinton to the occult. This artist paired Entering the Other Side with pieces by Bruce Nauman and Joseph Beuys in Seven Easy Pieces. This artist and their partner met in the middle of the Great Wall of China to end their relationship in the piece Lovers. A fight broke out when an (*) audience member held a gun to this artist's head in Rhythm 0. Lady Gaga helped popularize interest in this artist's performance at a 2010 MoMA retrospective in which this artist sat in silence across from museum visitors. For 10 points, name this Serbian "grandmother of performance art" whose work includes The Artist is Present.

tossups.question: A person of this sort advocated a new social policy in the three-part newspaper essay “Left Turn” and ran for parliament while jailed, which he described in the book My Fellow Prisoners. These people were forced to accept “new rules of the game” by a leader whose PhD thesis proposed “national champions” as an alternative to them. One of these people sued another of them after saying he coerced him into selling Sibneft shares. These people, who included a group called the “Seven Bankers,” first fully emerged after buying into the “loans-for-shares” scheme formed by Anatoly Chubais (“choo-BICE”). In 2022, one of these people sold a sports club to a consortium led by Todd Boehly (“bo-AY-lee”) when the Premier League disqualified him as a director of Chelsea. For 10 points, Mikhail Khodorkovsky and Roman Abramovich are what sort of businessmen who made fortunes when Russia’s economy was privatized?
tossups.answer: Russian oligarchs [or oligarkhi; prompt on Russians; prompt on businessmen, entrepreneurs, investors, or similar answers until “businessmen” is read] (Mikhail Khodorkovsky was the jailed writer. Vladimir Putin was the author of the PhD thesis. Boris Berezovsky sued Roman Abramovich over Sibneft.)

packet.name: Prelims 2. Northwestern A, Texas A, Virginia A

Vladimir Putin was the author of the PhD thesis. Boris Berezovsky sued Roman Abramovich over Sibneft.)

For 10 points, Mikhail Khodorkovsky and Roman Abramovich are what sort of businessmen who made fortunes when Russia's economy was privatized?

tossups.question: Lines in a book by this artist instruct the reader to obtain “13,000 grams of jealousy” to make “aphrodisiac recipes.” This artist led a series of 5-day long workshops called Cleaning the House where participants refrained from eating or speaking. In a piece, this artist leaned backwards holding a bow while another artist held an arrow to the string pointed at the heart. This artist played the knife game with 20 different knives in a debut piece in Edinburgh. In another piece, this artist (*) scrubbed down thousands of bloody cow bones over four days. This artist performed Thomas Lips and Entering the Other Side on the last two nights of the performance Seven Easy Pieces. Someone cut this artist’s neck and drank her blood during a performance involving 76 objects on a table. For 10 points, name this artist of Rhythm 0 who sat with different individual audience members for 736 total hours in The Artist Is Present, the Serbian “grandmother of performance art.”
tossups.question_sanitized: Lines in a book by this artist instruct the reader to obtain "13,000 grams of jealousy" to make "aphrodisiac recipes." This artist led a series of 5-day long workshops called Cleaning the House where participants refrained from eating or speaking. In a piece, this artist leaned backwards holding a bow while another artist held an arrow to the string pointed at the heart. This artist played the knife game with 20 different knives in a debut piece in Edinburgh. In another piece, this artist (*) scrubbed down thousands of bloody cow bones over four days. This artist performed Thomas Lips and Entering the Other Side on the last two nights of the performance Seven Easy Pieces. Someone cut this artist's neck and drank her blood during a performance involving 76 objects on a table. For 10 points, name this artist of Rhythm 0 who sat with different individual audience members for 736 total hours in The Artist Is Present, the Serbian "grandmother of performance art."

bonuses.leadin: In one performance art piece, one of these animals rested on the arm of an artist who repeatedly stepped over a tree while walking for three hours. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In one performance art piece, one of these animals rested on the arm of an artist who repeatedly stepped over a tree while walking for three hours. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these animals that were described as a “symbol of incarnation” by that artist, who created a performance art piece in which he whispered to one of them at the Galerie Schmela in Düsseldorf.
While performing How to Explain Pictures to a Dead Hare, Joseph Beuys coated his head in honey and the “leaf” of this metal, which was used by Gustav Klimt for the background of The Kiss.

This artist included How to Explain Pictures to a Dead Hare in a collection of Seven Easy Pieces dedicated to Susan Sontag. An audience member pointed a gun at this artist’s head during a performance of Rhythm 0.

answers: hares [accept rabbits or bunny or bunnies; accept How to Explain Pictures to a Dead Hare]
answers_sanitized: hares [accept rabbits or bunny or bunnies; accept How to Explain Pictures to a Dead Hare]

bonuses.leadin: Name some performance artists who covered themselves with food, for 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Name some performance artists who covered themselves with food, for 10 points each:
parts: This creator of Spirit Cooking included bread, wine, and honey among the objects available to participants in her performance Rhythm 0. She performed The Artist is Present in 2010.

This other artist covered himself in honey for the work 12 Square Meters, in which he sat in a filthy public toilet and allowed himself to be swarmed with flies. This artist led nine other artists from the Beijing East Village to lay down on top of a mountain summit in To Add One Meter to an Anonymous Mountain.
This American performance artist rolled around in sausages, fish, and other meat products in Meat Joy. She read from a scroll held in her own vagina in Interior Scroll.

This American performance artist rolled around in sausages, fish, and other meat products in Meat Joy. She read from a scroll held in her own vagina in Interior Scroll.

bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artist's longtime collaborator explored gender identity by displaying Polaroids showing himself dressed as a woman and smoking cigarettes in the exhibition "Renais Sense." For 10 points each:

Abramovic and Ulay held one of these objects for four minutes near Abramovic's chest in the collaborative piece, Rest Energy.

Abramovic and Ulay's final collaboration consisted of walking across this structure from opposite ends and meeting in the middle before parting ways. This 10,000-mile-long structure cannot actually be seen from space.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about multidisciplinary American artist Bruce Nauman, for 10 points each:
parts: Nauman spits a stream of water with hands aloft in a piece called Self-Portrait as this type of object. This type of object titles a Marcel Duchamp piece comprising a urinal signed “R. Mutt.”

As part of the series Seven Easy Pieces, this artist recreated a Nauman work in which the artist’s body presses against a wall. Onlookers applauded former collaborator Ulay surprising this artist during a silent 2010 piece.

A Nauman neon sign arranges the slogan “The true artist helps the world by revealing mystic truths” in this form. The artist of a work titled for this configuration remarked “et in Utah ego” upon viewing it.

bonuses.leadin: After a screening tour for a film depicting this action, its performers pledged not to talk about it for exactly 23 years after it first took place. For 10 points each:
parts: Describe this action that occurred in 1994 at a boathouse in Ardfin, a country estate in the Inner Hebrides. The K Foundation dissolved after performing this action.

In the Cremation Project, John Baldessari burned all of the objects of this kind in his possession that he made from 1953 to 1966. One of these objects created by Banksy was shredded during a Sotheby’s (“SUH-the-bee’s”) auction.
While performing Rhythm 5, this artist lost consciousness after setting fire to a wooden star and lying inside it. This artist’s Rhythm 10 involved jabbing at their own fingers with a knife.
answers: burning money [accept Watch the K Foundation Burn a Million Quid; accept any answers that indicate setting fire or burning British paper money]

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these performance artists who incorporated guns into their work.
parts: In her terrifying 1974 artwork Rhythm 0, this artist laid 72 items on a table including a pistol with one bullet, and invited the public to use it on her in any way they wished. Her other works include “The Artist is Present”.

In Shoot, this artist was shot in the shoulder with a .22 caliber rifle. He resigned his professorship from UCLA after a scandal in which a Master’s student played Russian Roulette for his live final project.

In one of his “Re-Enactments” this artist illegally bought a gun, pranced through the streets of Mexico City, was arrested, bribed the officers, and then convinced the corrupt po-po into reenacting the piece. This Belgian-born artist had a bunch of volunteers move a giant ice cube through Mexico City in The Paradox of Praxis One.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, name these performance artists who incorporated guns into their work.

In Shoot, this artist was shot in the shoulder with a .22 caliber rifle. He resigned his professorship from UCLA after a scandal in which a Master's student played Russian Roulette for his live final project.

In one of his "Re-Enactments" this artist illegally bought a gun, pranced through the streets of Mexico City, was arrested, bribed the officers, and then convinced the corrupt po-po into reenacting the piece. This Belgian-born artist had a bunch of volunteers move a giant ice cube through Mexico City in The Paradox of Praxis One.

bonuses.leadin: This economist developed the concept of Gross National Product as part of an interwar project at NBER. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Ukrainian-American economist who received the 1971 Nobel Prize for his work on economic growth. This economist also names 15-20 year production cycles.

The Kuznets curve predicts that this value will increase with industrialization and then decrease after a critical point. The Gini coefficient is a measure of this quantity, which is the primary subject of Thomas Piketty's Capital.
The Gini coefficient does not account for potential skew in this distribution, which can present as either a “demographic bulge” or “demographic dividend.”
answers: Simon Kuznets [or Simon Smith Kuznets; accept Semyon Abramovich Kuznets or Shimen Abramovich Kuznets; accept Kuznets cycles]
answers_sanitized: Simon Kuznets [or Simon Smith Kuznets; accept Semyon Abramovich Kuznets or Shimen Abramovich Kuznets; accept Kuznets cycles]

parts: Name this series of numbered performances. For its entry number “0,” the performer placed objects like a scalpel and a loaded gun on a table, stood still, and let the audience do whatever they wanted to her for six hours.
The Rhythm series was devised by this Serbian performance artist. In 2005, this woman performed works by Vito Acconci and Bruce Nauman live at the Guggenheim for her performance Seven Easy Pieces.

In 2010, Abramović sat stationary for a combined 736 hours in this New York City museum. Matisse’s The Dance and Picasso’s Les Demoiselles d'Avignon are housed in this museum which, unlike the Met, is in Midtown.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the performance art piece Seedbed for 10 points each.
parts: This artist described graphic sexual fantasies about guests over a loudspeaker while masturbating in a separate room in his Seedbed. Later in life, he went into architecture, designing buildings including the Murinsel in Graz, Austria.
This Serbian artist also performed a version of Seedbed in 2005. Her other pieces include The Artist is Present, in which she sat in a museum for 736 hours.

Seedbed took place in this city's Sonnabend Gallery. In Joseph Beuys' I Like America and America Likes Me, he flew into this city's John F. Kennedy Airport, then took an ambulance to a room where he stayed with a coyote for days before flying back out.

bonuses.leadin: During this artist’s Rhythm 0 [‘zero’], their clothes were cut away with razor blades and, eventually, a loaded gun was forced into their hand. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist, who in one work spent nineteen minutes breathing into their partner’s mouth, with whom they also stood naked either side of a doorway while people squeezed past them.
Rhythm 0, like many works of performance art, was an examination of the relationship of this entity with the artist. This entity is addressed by performers through the breaking of the fourth wall.

Allan Kaprow advocated these performative pieces which hinge on the unpredictability of the audience, such as when he presented 18 [of these things] in 6 Parts. John Cage had earlier experimented with ‘musical’ varieties of these pieces.

This woman recreated Pane’s The Conditioning as part of her series Seven Easy Pieces. She sat silently at a table with MoMA patrons in 2010’s The Artist is Present.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer the following about an assortment of easy pieces:
parts: The French princess Eugene Murat commissioned “Five Easy Pieces”, a set of piano duets by this composer. This composer wrote the Dumbarton Oaks concerto in his neoclassical phase.

“Six Easy Pieces” is the abridged version of a collection of lectures by this Caltech physicist. This physicist won the Nobel for his work on quantum electrodynamics and names diagrams used to model particle interactions.

This artist one-upped Feynman by presenting “Seven Easy Pieces” at the New York Guggenheim in 2005. Other major works by this “grandmother of performance art” have included “The Artist is Present” and “Rhythm Zero”.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, answer the following about an assortment of easy pieces:
parts_sanitized: The French princess Eugene Murat commissioned "Five Easy Pieces", a set of piano duets by this composer. This composer wrote the Dumbarton Oaks concerto in his neoclassical phase.

"Six Easy Pieces" is the abridged version of a collection of lectures by this Caltech physicist. This physicist won the Nobel for his work on quantum electrodynamics and names diagrams used to model particle interactions.

This artist one-upped Feynman by presenting "Seven Easy Pieces" at the New York Guggenheim in 2005. Other major works by this "grandmother of performance art" have included "The Artist is Present" and "Rhythm Zero".

bonuses.leadin: In one piece, this artist gave the audience objects including a gun and allowed them to do whatever they wanted to her. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this performance artist. In her most famous piece, “The Artist is Present,” she allowed people to sit across from her for however long they wanted to create an emotional connection.

In the performance art piece I Like America and America Likes Me, the Fluxus artist Joseph Beuys (boyz) flew to the US, lived in a small room with this wild animal for 8 hours a day for 3 days, and flew back to Germany.
In a eulogy for this artist, Allan Kaprow argued that his emphasis on the act of painting by the “dance of dripping” foreshadowed performance art.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In one piece, this artist gave the audience objects including a gun and allowed them to do whatever they wanted to her. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this performance artist. In her most famous piece, "The Artist is Present," she allowed people to sit across from her for however long they wanted to create an emotional connection.

In the performance art piece I Like America and America Likes Me, the Fluxus artist Joseph Beuys (boyz) flew to the US, lived in a small room with this wild animal for 8 hours a day for 3 days, and flew back to Germany.
In a eulogy for this artist, Allan Kaprow argued that his emphasis on the act of painting by the "dance of dripping" foreshadowed performance art.

bonuses.leadin: Several commentators compared Shia LaBeouf’s performance #IAMSORRY to this artist’s work. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Serbian performance artist, whose works include The Artist Is Present, in which visitors queued to sit opposite her in silence.

Abramović performed several works with this name, including offering audience members cake, paint, and 72 other objects to use on her. Charlie Parker and Dizzie Gillespie’s “Anthropology” is based on the George Gershwin song is “I got [this].”.
In 2005, Abramović re-enacted this artist’s performance How to Explain Pictures to a Dead Hare. A live coyote was involved in this artist’s 1974 ‘Action’ I Like America And America Likes Me.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Several commentators compared Shia LaBeouf's performance #IAMSORRY to this artist's work. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Serbian performance artist, whose works include The Artist Is Present, in which visitors queued to sit opposite her in silence.

Abramovic performed several works with this name, including offering audience members cake, paint, and 72 other objects to use on her. Charlie Parker and Dizzie Gillespie's "Anthropology" is based on the George Gershwin song is "I got [this].".
In 2005, Abramovic re-enacted this artist's performance How to Explain Pictures to a Dead Hare. A live coyote was involved in this artist's 1974 'Action' I Like America And America Likes Me.

bonuses.leadin: Answer these questions about contemporary art made by, or consisting of, women’s bodies, for 10 points each.
parts: This 20th-century French artist had naked women cover themselves in paint and roll all over a canvas to paint it in his “anthropometry” series. He created many monochrome works with a vivid “international…blue” color.

This Yugoslav-born performance artist let audiences handle 72 objects, including a loaded gun, as she sat for six hours in Rhythm 0. This frequent collaborator with Ulay sat motionless across from MoMA audiences who berated her one-by-one in The Artist is Present.
In this 1975 performance piece, Carolee Schneeman disrobed on stage, pulled a rolled-up piece of parchment out of her vagina, and read a speech on feminist theory which was printed on the parchment.
